IRONSITE // FIRST CALL

Limbach HoldingsNASDAQ · LMB

Prepared by Wyatt · 2026.04
01Snapshot

Who Limbach is

Public mechanical contractor, founded 1901, HQ Warrendale, PA. FY2024 revenue $518.8M, FY2025 guidance $650-680M, ~1,400 team members across 20 offices in the eastern US. Six verticals: healthcare, industrial and manufacturing, data centers, life science, higher education, cultural and entertainment. Majority of craft workforce is under collective bargaining. Business is shifting fast toward Owner Direct Relationships (ODR), which hit 76.6% of Q3 2025 revenue.

Revenue FY24
$518.8M +0.5% YoY
Revenue FY25e
$650-680M
Adj EBITDA FY24
$63.7M +36.1%
Gross margin FY24
27.8%
Team
~1,400
Offices
20 · Eastern US
HQ
Warrendale, PA
Founded
1901
Ownership
Public · LMB
ODR · Q3'25
76.6%
Craft
Union majority
Verticals
6 · MEP owner-direct

Notable recent moves: acquired Pioneer Power (industrial mechanical, Upper Midwest) for $66.1M on Jul 1, 2025, adding ~$120M annualized revenue from 2026. Consolidated Mechanical (KY/MI/IL) was added in 2024 for $23M. ACME Industrial Piping and Industrial Air were acquired earlier in 2024. A national healthcare owner engagement across 20 locations produced $12M in capital projects at four sites in Q3 2025.

Sources: FY2024 results (Mar 10, 2025) · Q3 2025 transcript · Pioneer Power acquisition · FY2024 10-K

02Why now, three signals

Three public signals from the last 12 months

Q3 2025 call: gross margin guide cut to 25.5-26.5%, T&M is ~1/3 of ODR.

Nov 27, 2025 · Q3 2025 earnings call transcript · Strong
→ Camera-on-helmet footage ties billed T&M hours to observed install activity. On a $180-200M annual T&M run-rate, even a 50 bps billing-accuracy gain is real money. Defends gross margin in the fastest-growing segment.

Pioneer Power closes: four industrial mechanical acquisitions in 18 months.

Jul 1, 2025 · Acquisition press release · Strong
→ Four field-ops cultures, four tool stacks, different craft locals. Per-worker productivity data is the fastest way to normalize output across the acquired companies without a multi-year ERP lift. Wearable rollout is days, not quarters.

Nick Angerosa promoted to EVP National Customer Solutions (from Pres, Southeast).

Jan 13, 2026 · Limbach press release · Strong
→ Fresh EVP, ops background, national charter, stated focus on data center and industrial growth. The announcement itself names "tools, data, and insights" as the equipping mandate. Short political window to place a category bet.
03The buyer

Who to call, and why them

Nick Angerosa

EVP, National Customer Solutions · Limbach

Primary13 yrs at LimbachEx-Pres, SE Region

Appointed to the new national role on Jan 13, 2026. Came up through regional operations, not sales. His public charter integrates operational insight across national customer accounts, with an explicit tilt to data centers and industrial.

Route in: LinkedIn note referencing the appointment plus one line on what Ironsite's first-week output would look like for a Pioneer Power or CMI crew. 15 minutes, one crew, one scope.

LinkedIn: search "Nick Angerosa Limbach" · Appointment release

Jayme Brooks

EVP & CFO · Limbach

BackupMargin defense angle

Q3 2025 guidance cut gross margin from 28-29% to 25.5-26.5%. Per-worker productivity data is a margin-defense tool that a CFO can expense against FY26 EBITDA targets.

Limbach leadership page

04Mocked first-week output

What Ironsite's dashboard would show after week one

Illustrative All names, dates, and specific numbers in this section are mock data. This is a static picture of what Ironsite's output would look like for a crew of this size and trade, built from published field-productivity research. Percentage ranges used below are cited in the footnote at the end of the section.
IRONSITE // CREW WEEK 01
IND-MECH · Healthcare central plant · Indianapolis, IN · Mon-Fri

M. KowalskiJourneyman PipefitterUA 440548.529%
A. OkaforWelderUA 440440.034%
J. BellamyApprentice PipefitterUA 440550.022%
R. OrtizHelperNon-UA545.019%

WorkerInstall → Prep → Wait → Coord → ReworkBilled hrs

50.0
T. Novak
50.0

48.5

40.0
J. Bellamy
50.0
R. Ortiz
45.0
Install Prep & stage Wait Coord Rework

Ranges used: install 25-35%, prep/stage 35-45%, wait/coord 10-20% combined, rework 5-10%. Anchored to McKinsey MGI, Reinventing Construction (2017) and FMI rework research. The mock shows ~28% average install, ~39% prep, ~15% wait, ~14% coord, ~6% rework.

T&M substantiation · scope hours observed vs. billed
ScopeBilledObs. installObs. prepDeltaFlag
Chilled water pipe (2nd-flr header)88.027.546.0+14.5 stage-heavyReview
VRF riser weld-ups56.020.520.0+15.5 wait/coordReview
Isolation valve install24.08.010.5+5.5 reworkDispute risk
Mechanical room GC cleanup12.04.07.5~alignedOK
Safety flags · week 01
Day / Time
Type
What Ironsite saw
Mon 09:14
PPE
Helper (R. Ortiz) <6 ft from active welding without screen; flash-risk event logged.
Tue 13:48
Lift
Scissor lift extended with a 3rd worker on deck; exceeds manufacturer capacity for this model.
Wed 10:22
Proximity
Pipefitter within swing radius of unattended overhead load during rigging.
Thu 15:10
Hot work
Fire watch left position 6 min before burn-cool timer expired.

What a super would do next week

  • Pull Tue 13:48 clip for toolbox talk; retrain on scissor-lift ratings with the whole crew.
  • On the VRF riser scope, pre-stage weld-up components Sunday night so Tue/Wed aren't front-loaded with prep. Target install share back above 30%.
  • Have Reyes walk the isolation-valve rework with apprentice Bellamy before submitting that line on the next T&M ticket; either pull hours back or document cause of rework.
05Objections

What Angerosa will push back on, and the honest answer

01"Our craft is majority UA and IBEW. Worker-mounted cameras are a bargaining-unit fight we're not looking to pick."
Fair, and worth naming on the first call. The clean path is a pilot on a single crew with the local's rep in the room, footage retained by Limbach, and written scope limits on use (T&M substantiation, safety review, no performance discipline). Most recent wearable-camera pilots at union mechanical contractors have landed on that kind of written MOU. Worth asking UA 440 before any rollout, not after.
02"Pioneer Power and CMI already run their own foreman tracking and we just bolted Procore and safety cameras onto the eastern offices. What does wearable add that we don't have?"
Procore captures what was planned and logged. Fixed safety cameras cover fixed locations. Neither gives you per-worker install time on a cost-plus scope, and neither produces a clip you can send a healthcare owner in an hour when a T&M ticket gets disputed. Wearable is specifically for the field labor gap between the plan and the invoice.
03"We cut gross margin guidance this quarter because of mix and acquisition integration, not field productivity. Adding a hardware line item doesn't help in 2026."
Acquired companies is exactly where the ROI shows up. On a single Pioneer Power crew, moving install share from a week-01 baseline of ~28% to 33% on T&M scopes is worth more than the hardware cost for the year. The pilot is small. The downside is a refund and 90 days of dashboards. The upside is a normalized per-worker baseline across four acquisitions.
06First touch

Two drafts for the first message to Angerosa

LinkedIn DMDraft · not sent
Nick, congrats on the national role. Saw the Jan 13 release and that the Q3 call flagged ~1/3 of ODR sits in T&M. I put together a sample first-week Ironsite readout for one industrial mechanical crew at a healthcare plant. 15 minutes to walk through it? Happy to send the link either way.
62 words
EmailDraft · not sent
Subject · T&M substantiation for Pioneer Power and CMI crewsNick, Your Q3 call put T&M work at about a third of ODR. Limbach has a margin-defense story to write there, and the Pioneer Power and CMI integrations are the fastest place to produce a normalized per-worker baseline. I put together a first-week Ironsite readout for one industrial mechanical crew as a sample of what that looks like. Static, no commitment. 15 minutes the week of May 5 to walk through it?
88 words
07Why I built this

Max, the argument for hiring me is that I will do this every week for whichever account is next. I grew up on Menemsha Harbor working scrap metal summers for a small industrial recycler, and I run cold BD at getcracked.io now. I have active threads open with Thalheimer and Weitsman on the scrap side. What you get here is not a dashboard or a deck. It is one page per account, built from public sources, in a voice a field super won't roll their eyes at. This is the sample. Tell me the next name and I'll turn another one in four hours.

Wyatt [lastname] · [email] · [LinkedIn URL]
